fact: October 3 is exactly 276 days into the year, which means there are only 89 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now, hiding up to 10,000 acorns each to survive winter!
The sun is setting about 2 minutes earlier each day around October 3, making bedtime feel like it comes super fast.
Maple trees are pumping sugar down to their roots right now, which is why their leaves turn those crazy bright red and orange colors.
October 3 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, when the zodiac scales are supposed to be perfectly balanced - just like how day and night were almost equal a few weeks ago!
Monarch butterflies are flying over 2,000 miles south right now, using magnetic fields in the Earth to navigate like tiny living compasses.
The morning dew gets heavier around this time because cool air can't hold as much water vapor, so it literally falls out of the sky onto grass.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, October 3 means spring is in full swing and baby animals are being born everywhere!
Spiders seem to appear in houses more around early October because they're actually trying to find warm places to spend the winter.
The moon looks bigger and more orange when it rises around this time of year because you're looking at it through thicker, dustier air.
Apple trees are at their peak ripeness right now in northern climates, and a single tree can produce enough apples to make 20 pies!
October 3 is roughly 12 days after the autumn equinox, which means we're officially sliding into the darker half of the year in the north.
